message:
perf(session): optimize save hotpath and tree traversal for large sessions

Eliminate the most significant session save bottleneck: compaction entries
no longer unconditionally force full-file rewrite. Previously, any new
compaction entry triggered O(file-size) rewrite of the entire session file;
now compaction entries are eligible for incremental append like regular
entries. Full rewrite triggers are retained for: first save, header dirty,
checkpoint interval, and defensive persisted-count mismatch. This yields
a measured 3.14x speedup in save_ms at 5M tokens (359ms -> 114ms).

Tree traversal optimizations for branched and linear sessions:
- Add fast path in get_path_to_entry() for linear sessions: directly
  slice entries[..=idx] instead of walking parent chain, O(1) lookup
  via entry_index instead of building a parent map
- Remove build_parent_map() entirely — parent lookups now use direct
  entry access via get_entry() instead of pre-computing a full HashMap
- Pre-allocate build_children_map() HashMap with session entry count
- Optimize list_leaves() to use a HashSet<&str> of parent IDs instead
  of building full children map then checking for empty children
- Optimize get_children() to filter entries directly instead of building
  children map for single lookup
- Add fast path in entries_for_current_path() for linear sessions
- Add fast path in to_messages_for_current_path() for linear sessions

Code deduplication and allocation reduction:
- Extract append_model_message_for_entry() to deduplicate message
  conversion logic used in compaction and non-compaction paths
- Extract to_messages_from_path() generic over index-based entry access
  to serve both linear (direct slice) and branched (path_entries vec)
- Combine entry_preview() + count_messages_on_path() into single-walk
  path_preview_and_message_count(), halving parent-chain traversals
- Pre-allocate visited HashSets with capped capacity min(entries.len, 128)
- Use HashSet for current_branch_ids in build_fork_map for O(1) lookup

Test update:
- Rename test_compaction_entry_forces_full_rewrite to
  test_compaction_entry_uses_incremental_append with updated assertions
  verifying incremental behavior (persisted_count=3, appends_since=1,
  line count=4)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
